primary_source.title: B2B Sales Benchmark Research Finds Some Pipeline Surprises

verifications.evidence_quote: On average, 13% of leads convert to opportunities and the average time for conversion is 84 days (Salesforce/Implisit analysis). Lead to Customer sits at 2 to 6%.
search_queries_tried: '3% of leads' 'convert to deals' benchmark B2B pipeline metrics

credibility_signal_for_practitioner.reason: Stated as a hard benchmark ('only like 3% of leads turn into deals') but published B2B benchmarks put lead-to-customer at 2-6% (depending on channel and ACV) and lead-to-opportunity at ~13% — meaning '3% to deals' is roughly within the lead-to-customer range but stated too narrowly and prescriptively.

verifications.notes: The 3% figure is approximately within the published 2-6% lead-to-customer range, but the practitioner overstates it as a universal benchmark when in reality it's industry/channel-dependent. Treat as partially supported in spirit, but the specific number lacks an authoritative single source. Marked contradicted because the practitioner is asserting a single benchmark where no consensus single number exists.

primary_source.title: How to Improve Lead-to-Opportunity Conversion Rate

verifications.evidence_quote: A B2B SaaS company implemented automated lead scoring and reduced response time from 24 hours to under 5 minutes, resulting in their SQL Conversion Rate jumping from 15% to 25% and a 40% increase in qualified opportunities within a single quarter. 360 Learning saw a 40% increase in conversion rates and 97% assignment accuracy using lead scoring with automated routing.
search_queries_tried: 'lead to opportunity' conversion improvement '40%' '60%' lead scoring

credibility_signal_for_practitioner.reason: Range plausibility-confirmed by case studies showing 40% lifts; the '60%' upper bound is more aggressive but within the realm of reported case studies. Not contradicted.

verifications.notes: The 40% figure has multiple case-study confirmations (360 Learning, B2B SaaS examples). The 60% upper bound is harder to substantiate from a primary source — most published case studies cluster around 28-40%. Marked partially supported.

verifications.notes: Substance of claim is confirmed. The 'recently came out with an update' framing is questionable since negative ranges have been part of the new tool since launch in 2025 — but the practitioner is internal HubSpot product and may be referring to a specific recent change to total-score handling. Charitable read: confirmed at 0.80.

primary_source.title: Understand the lead scoring tool

verifications.evidence_quote: Score decay intervals can be set to every 1, 3, 6, or 12 months, with each month equal to 30 days. To set up score decay, which automatically reduces a score based on how long ago a scored event occurred, you toggle the Decay scores switch on and select the percentage to reduce the score by, and the period of time that must pass to reduce the score.
search_queries_tried: HubSpot lead scoring 'score decay' 30 days percentage engagement degrade 2025

credibility_signal_for_practitioner.reason: The CAPABILITY (native score decay with user-selectable percentage and time interval) is fully confirmed by HubSpot KB. The PARAMETER VALUES the practitioner gives ('30%/30 days', '20-50% range') are reasonable example settings within HubSpot's configurable space, but they are not platform-canonical defaults — they are the practitioner's prescriptive recommendation.

verifications.notes: HubSpot KB confirms: (1) score decay exists as a native feature post-Aug-2025, (2) user-configurable percentage, (3) time intervals limited to 1, 3, 6, or 12 months — note that HubSpot's smallest interval is '1 month (30 days)', so the practitioner's '30 days' framing is correct. The specific 30% / 20-50% range is the practitioner's prescription, not a HubSpot default. Status: partially_supported — capability and intervals confirmed; specific percentages are practitioner recommendation.
